expectations.text: Response includes a floor, target, and stretch price (three-tier price range)
expectations.evidence: Clear summary table: Floor $38,000 / Target $48,000 / Stretch $62,000. Named explicitly throughout.

expectations.text: Response considers at least 3 of the 4 pricing lenses: cost, market, value, risk
expectations.evidence: All 4 lenses have dedicated sections with headers: 'Lens 1: Cost', 'Lens 2: Market', 'Lens 3: Value', 'Lens 4: Risk'. Each includes detailed analysis.

expectations.text: Response analyzes the $25K budget hint and whether it is below, at, or above market
expectations.evidence: Dedicated section 'How to Handle the $25K Budget Signal' with 3 options. Also states: 'The client's $25K budget signal is at or below your cost floor' and 'should not anchor your pricing.'

expectations.text: Response recommends a specific pricing structure (project fee, phased, retainer, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Recommends phased structure: $8K discovery (also functions as paid pilot) + $35-48K build + $2-4K/month optional retainer. Explains why phased is better than lump-sum.

expectations.text: Response explicitly states assumptions (about scope, timeline, team, or complexity)
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Assumptions' section with 9 numbered items covering developer skill level, web vs native app, integration requirements, timeline, client responsiveness, hosting costs, post-launch support, hourly rate basis, and revenue estimates.

expectations.text: Response provides specific dollar amounts, not just generic advice
expectations.evidence: Specific amounts throughout: cost floor $28K-$52K, market range $20K-$80K, value capture $32K-$87K, final floor $38K, target $48K, stretch $62K, phase 1 $8K, phase 2 $35-48K, retainer $2-4K/month.
